title: Split FRAMEWORK.md into docs/ folder structure
note:
FRAMEWORK.md is getting large (200+ lines). Split into:

~/.openclaw/workspace/docs/
├── framework.md   ← Core rules (framework selection, anti-patterns, build tool rules)
├── roles.md       ← Agent roles, permissions, merge checklist
├── routes.md      ← Active routes manifest + testing procedure
├── stack.md       ← Tech stack, dependencies, gotchas
├── workflow.md    ← Dev→Prod workflow, automation rules, OpenCode usage
└── sources.md     ← External documentation references (SOURCES.md mirror)

FRAMEWORK.md becomes a lean index pointing to each file.

Move relevant sections from FRAMEWORK.md into these files. Update any references.

history.details: Created from Kanban sense check — FRAMEWORK.md needs to be split before it becomes unwieldy
Status changed from backlog to in_progress
Ghost dispatch — in_progress with no activity updates. Reset to backlog.
Picked up by heartbeat — splitting FRAMEWORK.md into docs/ folder

summary: Split FRAMEWORK.md into docs/ folder structure. Created 6 files: docs/framework.md (framework selection, build tool rules, anti-patterns, gotchas), docs/roles.md (operations engine, agent communication, security rules, component rules), docs/routes.md (routes manifest + testing procedure), docs/stack.md (tech stack, dependencies), docs/workflow.md (dev/prod workflow, git rules, merge checklist), docs/sources.md (external docs references). Rewrote FRAMEWORK.md as a lean index with Four-Repository Structure, Local Directory Mapping, and links to all docs/ files. Committed locally (f5f7c3f). Push failed — SSH key id_ed25519_spacemonkey missing, repo access issue with spacemonkey-home account.
